hierarchy_rules: Labels/Overlines: uppercase text-xs tracking-[0.2em] font-mono text-gray-400

rules: No pure #000000 - use #0A0A0A for deepest background.
Primary CTA should use Signal Red (#FF3B30) for alerts, or white outline for secondary.
Explicitly set text color on hover (e.g., hover:bg-white/10 hover:text-white).

layout_and_spacing.grid_classes: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 md:gap-6
rules: Every pixel utilized, h-full on children for uniform stretching.
Containers with borders MUST have internal padding (p-6).
Optical alignment: if an element has a round icon on left, use extra padding to visually center text.

surfaces_and_borders.cards: Flat background (#121212), 1px solid border (border-[#2A2A2A]), no shadow.
surfaces_and_borders.header: Crystal Glass - backdrop-blur-xl bg-[#0A0A0A]/70 border-b border-[#2A2A2A] z-50
surfaces_and_borders.data_widgets: Grid Borders - expose the skeleton, subtle borders on right and bottom of grid items.

icons.usage: Use Phosphor icons (Duotone or Regular) for dense data visualizations and UI adornments.

charts.usage: Use Recharts for epidemiological curves and daily case timelines. Ensure lines are bright neon (Red/Blue) with dark grid lines (stroke='#333').

maps.usage: Use react-leaflet for interactive maps. MUST use dark mode tiles: 'https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/dark_all/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png'. Heatmaps must use red/orange gradients.

ui_components.usage: Use Shadcn components heavily customized to match the Swiss Brutalist/Dark Control Room aesthetic. Strip default Vercel-look borders, use sharp or max 4px border-radius (rounded-sm).

media_and_images.hero_overlay: bg-black/60 to ensure map data pops.

image_urls.description: Abstract red data explosion - use for alert sections or AI insights background with bg-black/80 overlay.
Lab research image - use for about/methodology sections.

logos.instruction_to_main_agent:
CRITICAL: Do NOT use placeholder image URLs for WHO, CDC, ECDC logos. Create typographic logos using plain text (e.g.,
WHO
) or use Phosphor Icons to represent institutions.

animations_and_interactions.transitions: Use specific properties: transition: transform 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease, opacity 0.2s ease.
animations_and_interactions.marquee: Use react-fast-marquee for a slow 'Editorial Ribbon' of breaking news/alerts across the top or middle of the dashboard. Slow, smooth drift. Red text on dark background.

accessibility_and_testing.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='map-view-toggle').
accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Ensure text over red/dark backgrounds passes APCA/WCAG ratios. Use text-shadow if overlaid on the map.

instructions_to_main_agent: NEVER USE PLACEHOLDER IMAGES (e.g., via.placeholder.com). Use the provided Unsplash URLs or CSS/Text for logos.
Implement the Leaflet map immediately as it's the core functionality. Use CartoDB Dark Matter tiles.
Data structure must support multiple countries, confirmed cases, active outbreaks, deaths, and timestamps.

Include placeholder spaces for Google AdSense (e.g., a div with 'Advertisement' label and specific dimensions like w-full h-32 bg-[#121212] border border-[#2A2A2A] flex items-center justify-center text-gray-500 font-mono text-xs).
Ensure the dashboard is mobile-first, but optimized for large desktop screens (command-center feel).
No Inter font for headings. Use Chivo.
